expectations.text: Opening does NOT use generic proposal language (Dear, Thank you for your interest, Please find attached)
expectations.evidence: none of the banned patterns appeared

expectations.text: Proposal names a specific RP service tier (Mirror, Map, or Territory)
Proposal includes specific pricing ($-amount) within documented ranges
Uses Say Why framing (not generic 'brand strategy' or 'content marketing')
expectations.evidence: Names 'Say Why' as practice and frames work as narrative excavation, rejects brand refresh

expectations.text: Opener names the client's specific narrative gap/pattern (diagnostic), not generic appreciation
expectations.evidence: Names 'three different voices' and diagnoses narrative fragmentation

expectations.text: Follows the 6-section structure (Opening, Understanding, Proposed Engagement, Investment, Why This Approach, Next Steps)
expectations.evidence: Uses alternate section labels; 'Why I'm the right person' replaces 'Why This Approach' and lacks canonical 'Understanding' label

expectations.text: Names concrete deliverables (e.g. Bilingual Dashboard, facilitation sessions, specific asset formats)
expectations.evidence: Narrative core document, voice architecture, channel audit, one-page summary

expectations.text: Closes with ONE clear next step (not a menu)
expectations.evidence: Sequential 'reply with confirmation' as primary action

expectations.text: Names Map tier specifically (not Mirror or Territory)
Pricing falls in Map full sprint range ($6,000-$12,000), within Delta's $10K cap
References Delta's specific gap (three different organizations speaking)
expectations.evidence: 'speaking in three different voices' named directly

expectations.text: Names the Bilingual Dashboard as a deliverable
expectations.evidence: No mention of 'Bilingual Dashboard' anywhere in the document
